data.description:
## Story S2: Per-session isolation via session-start hook + session_id sanitization + CI fallback

### Description
**What**: Extend the DSO session-start hook to capture the Claude Code `session_id` from hook input, sanitize to `[a-f0-9-]+` (non-conforming inputs are hashed into a conforming key), and write `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-test-run/.current-session`. `test-run.sh` reads this file to scope all state under `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-test-run/<session_id>/`. When `.current-session` is missing, the launcher falls back to `$GITHUB_RUN_ID` → `$CI_JOB_ID` → `sha256(cwd)[:16]` in that precedence order. Never uses a silent shared default.
**Why**: Cross-session concurrency (two Claude sessions running tests simultaneously in different worktrees or the same worktree) requires per-session state keying. CI runs are not Claude sessions and need a deterministic fallback.
**Scope**:
- IN: session-start hook update, session_id sanitizer, `.current-session` marker, per-session state dir layout, CI fallback chain
- OUT: same-session duplicate-launch guard (S3), cleanup of orphaned state dirs (operational — not in this epic)

### Done Definitions
- When this story is complete, session-start hook writes the sanitized session_id to `${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-test-run/.current-session`
  ← Satisfies: "session-start hook captures the Claude Code session_id, sanitizes to [a-f0-9-]+, and writes ${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/dso-test-run/.current-session"
- When this story is complete, two Claude sessions with different session_ids invoke `test-run start` simultaneously without shared-state collision (state dirs are distinct)
  ← Satisfies: "Two Claude sessions running in different worktrees (or the same worktree) can invoke test-run start simultaneously without any shared-state collision"
- When this story is complete, a CI run (no `.current-session` present) derives a session key from `$GITHUB_RUN_ID`, `$CI_JOB_ID`, or `sha256(cwd)` in that precedence order
  ← Satisfies: "When .current-session is missing (CI runs, session-start hook failed to fire, upgraded plugin with stale hooks), the launcher falls back to a deterministic key derived from $GITHUB_RUN_ID, $CI_JOB_ID, or a cwd-hash in that precedence order — never silently using a shared default"
- When this story is complete, a session_id containing `/`, `..`, or whitespace is rejected-and-hashed rather than used directly in a path
  ← Satisfies: "sanitizes it to [a-f0-9-]+ (rejecting any input containing /, .., whitespace, or path-traversal sequences — fall back to a hash of the raw session_id when the input is non-conforming)"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

### Considerations
- [Security] path-traversal sanitization is a security-relevant boundary — verify with adversarial inputs in unit tests
- [Reliability] session-start hook registration must survive plugin upgrades; if hook fails to fire, the CI fallback chain keeps the launcher functional
## Escalation Policy
**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
data.title: As a DSO agent, my session gets an isolated state dir via session-start hook with session_id sanitization and CI fallback
